title: Getting Started with GitHub Copilot Chat

steps.title: Welcome to Copilot Chat
steps.instruction: This is the GitHub Copilot Chat panel. It's where you'll interact with Copilot to get code suggestions, explanations, and more.

steps.title: Choose your model
steps.instruction: Click the model selector to pick which AI model Copilot uses. Different models have different strengths.

steps.title: Type a prompt
steps.instruction: Type your question or request in the chat input at the bottom. Try: Explain what this function does

steps.title: Send your message
steps.instruction: Press Enter or click the send button to submit your prompt.

steps.title: Read the response
steps.instruction: Copilot responds with a detailed explanation. Notice how it uses code formatting and clear language.

steps.title: Continue the conversation
steps.instruction: You can ask follow-up questions — Copilot remembers the conversation context. Try: <kbd>Can you add error handling to it?</kbd>

messages.content: This function calculateTotal takes an array of cart items and returns the sum of their prices, applying any active discounts.
Can you add error handling to it?

steps.title: Attach context
steps.instruction: Use the attach button to add files, selections, or other context to your prompt. This helps Copilot give more accurate answers.

messages.content: This function calculateTotal takes an array of cart items and returns the sum of their prices, applying any active discounts.

steps.title: Lab complete!
steps.instruction: You now know the basics of GitHub Copilot Chat. In the next lab, we'll explore slash commands and agents.

messages.content:
You're welcome! You're off to a great start. Here are some tips for your next steps:

• Use /explain to explain selected code
• Use /fix to suggest bug fixes
• Use /tests to generate test cases
• Use @workspace to ask questions about your entire project

Happy coding! 🚀
